title: GNU General Public License v3.0
text: package licensed terms GNU General Public License version 3 (GPL-3). full text available https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.html. biljouR independent re-implementation BILJOU forest water balance model (Granier et al. 1999) research teaching. produced endorsed INRAE.

title: The BILJOU forest water balance model in R
text: model integrates, daily time step, soil water balance ΔW=P−−T−Eu−D\Delta W = P - - T - Eu - D P rainfall, rainfall interception, T overstorey transpiration, Eu understorey + soil evaporation D drainage. Drainage obtained difference / cascade soil layers.

title: The BILJOU forest water balance model in R
text: LAI phenology. Evergreen stands keep constant LAI; deciduous stands ramp 0 lai_max 30 days budburst back 0 30 days leaf fall. Transpiration. T=r(LAI)⋅PETT = r(LAI) \cdot PET, r=0.125⋅LAIr = 0.125 \cdot LAI LAI≤6LAI \le 6 r=0.75r = 0.75 . TT reduced 20 % intercepted water (wet canopy) scaled REW/REWcREW/REW_c water stress (REWc=0.4REW_c = 0.4). Interception. Th=exp⁡(+b⋅R/R0+c⋅P+d⋅P2)Th = \exp(+ b \cdot R/R_0 + c \cdot P + d \cdot P^2), =P−ThIn = P - Th (Aussenac 1968), stand-type coefficients; R/R0=100⋅exp⁡(−k⋅LAI)R/R_0 = 100 \cdot \exp(-k \cdot LAI). Understorey/soil evaporation. Eu=PET⋅exp⁡(−k⋅LAI)Eu = PET \cdot \exp(-k \cdot LAI), limited upper-layer water. Soil & drainage. layer refills microporosity fraction bypasses quickly macroporosity; water field capacity drains next layer. Root uptake weighted fine-root fraction current relative extractable water.

title: The BILJOU forest water balance model in R
code: library(biljouR) data(meteo_hesse)  soil <- biljou_soil(   ewm   = c(70, 70, 40),                 # mm extractable water per layer   roots = c(0.6, 0.3, 0.1),              # fine-root fractions   macro = c(0.3, 0.2, 0.1),              # optional macroporosity   micro = c(0.7, 0.8, 0.9)               # optional microporosity ) soil #> <biljou_soil>: 3 layer(s) #>  layer ewm_mm root_frac bypass_frac #>      1     70       0.6         0.3 #>      2     70       0.3         0.2 #>      3     40       0.1         0.1 #> Total extractable water (EWM): 180 mm  run <- biljou_run(meteo_hesse, soil, lai_max = 6,                   forest_type = "broadleaved",                   budburst = 110, leaf_fall = 300) run #> <biljou_run>: 365 days, broadleaved stand, LAI_max = 6  #> Period: 2003-01-01 to 2003-12-31  #> Totals (mm): rain=652  interception=45  transpiration=254  understorey=165  drainage=194 #> Min REW=0.09  stress days (REW<0.40)=133

title: The BILJOU forest water balance model in R
code: biljou_indices(run) #>   year NJstress  Istress DEBstress    min_rew  is_1999  rain transpiration #> 1 2003      113 58.67041       188 0.09185474 23.46816 232.8      254.4782 #>   understorey interception drainage       et #> 1    40.38769     45.08485 7.084437 339.9507 biljou_annual_balance(run) #>   year  rain interception throughfall transpiration understorey       et #> 1 2003 651.8     45.08485    606.7151      254.4782    164.6177 464.1807 #>   drainage #> 1 193.6184

title: The BILJOU forest water balance model in R
code: plot(run)

title: The BILJOU forest water balance model in R
text: BILJOU calibrated Penman PET. already :
code: penman_pet(tmean = 18, rg = 22, wind = 2, rh = 65,            doy = 180, latitude = 48.6, altitude = 250) #> [1] 5.081286

title: The BILJOU forest water balance model in R
text: ggplot2 installed, package draws families figures online tool. multi-year run: Inter-annual statistics day--year (base R, extra dependency): reproduce BILJOU-style maps, run model grid points export sf/terra. SAFRAN data (Meteo-France, 8 km, free) read safran_to_meteo(); see README download links.
code: # multi-year chronicle / faceted time series biljou_plot_timeseries(run, vars = c("ETP", "ETR", "transpiration", "REW"))  # overlay years by day-of-year, with a median curve (REW shown with its threshold) biljou_plot_overlay(run, var = "REW", stat = "median") stats <- biljou_doy_stats(run, var = "REW", probs = c(0.1, 0.9)) head(stats)   # doy, n, mean, sd, median, q10, q90 grid <- biljou_run_grid(points, meteo = meteo_per_point,                         soil = biljou_soil(140), lai_max = 5,                         forest_type = "broadleaved",                         budburst = 110, leaf_fall = 300,                         indicators = "NJstress") r <- biljou_grid_to_raster(grid, indicator = "NJstress", year = 2003) terra::plot(r)

title: The BILJOU forest water balance model in R
text: constants described qualitatively public BILJOU documentation. choices made (macro/micro split, root-uptake weighting, understorey coefficient, interception clamping) transparent documented source, validated official tool production use.

title: Cartographie de la sécheresse avec SAFRAN
text: Le miroir NetCDF par variable (INRAE, Recherche Data Gouv, DOI 10.57745/BAZ12C) est la source la plus pratique : ne télécharge que les trois variables utiles au modèle — pluie liquide PRELIQ_Q, pluie solide PRENEI_Q et ETP de référence ETP_Q. Le code ci-dessous nécessite une connexion réseau et les packages jsonlite et terra ; il n’est donc pas exécuté à la compilation de la vignette. Astuce : les points SAFRAN d’origine sont repérés en Lambert II (LAMBX/LAMBY, en hectomètres). Pour les passer en lon/lat : sf::st_transform(sf::st_as_sf(pts, coords = c("x","y"), crs = 27572), 4326) après avoir multiplié LAMBX/LAMBY par 100. La géométrie exacte peut varier selon le produit : inspectez un fichier avec terra::rast() avant l’extraction.
code: # Lister les fichiers du jeu (API Dataverse) files_dispo <- safran_dataverse_files() head(files_dispo)  # Télécharger uniquement les variables nécessaires files <- safran_download(variables = c("PRELIQ_Q", "PRENEI_Q", "ETP_Q"),                          dest_dir = "safran")  # Points de grille d'intérêt (lon/lat WGS84) points <- data.frame(id  = c("p1", "p2", "p3"),                      lon = c(6.0, 6.2, 6.4),                      lat = c(48.6, 48.7, 48.8))  # Extraire la météo par point depuis les NetCDF meteo_list <- safran_nc_to_meteo(files, points)  # Lancer le modèle sur la grille grille <- biljou_run_grid(points, meteo = meteo_list,                           soil = biljou_soil(140), lai_max = 5,                           forest_type = "broadleaved",                           budburst = 110, leaf_fall = 300,                           indicators = "NJstress")

title: Cartographie de la sécheresse avec SAFRAN
text: Pour que la vignette tourne partout (sans réseau ni gros téléchargement), remplace SAFRAN par une météo synthétique sur une petite grille régulière, avec un gradient de pluie d’ouest en est. La logique de bout en bout est identique à celle du cas réel. peut classer les points par sévérité, exactement comme l’outil classe les années :
code: # Grille régulière 4 x 3 points grid_xy <- expand.grid(lon = seq(6.0, 6.6, by = 0.2),                        lat = seq(48.6, 48.8, by = 0.1)) points <- data.frame(id = paste0("c", seq_len(nrow(grid_xy))), grid_xy)  # Une année de référence + gradient de pluie : plus sec vers l'est data(meteo_hesse) meteo_fun <- function(id) {   i <- match(id, points$id)   # facteur de pluie 1.2 (ouest) -> 0.5 (est)   f <- 1.2 - 0.7 * (points$lon[i] - min(points$lon)) /               (max(points$lon) - min(points$lon))   transform(meteo_hesse, rain = rain * f) }  # Modèle sur toute la grille grille <- biljou_run_grid(points, meteo = meteo_fun,                           soil = biljou_soil(140), lai_max = 5,                           forest_type = "broadleaved",                           budburst = 110, leaf_fall = 300,                           indicators = c("NJstress", "Istress", "min_rew"),                           verbose = FALSE) print(grille) #> <biljou_grid>: 12 points, 1 year(s), 3 indicator(s) #> Indicators: NJstress, Istress, min_rew  #>     id lon  lat year indicator        value #> 1   c1 6.0 48.6 2003  NJstress  98.00000000 #> 2   c1 6.0 48.6 2003   Istress  49.98710783 #> 3   c1 6.0 48.6 2003   min_rew   0.08972377 #> 4   c2 6.2 48.6 2003  NJstress 115.00000000 #> 5   c2 6.2 48.6 2003   Istress  62.24641800 #> 6   c2 6.2 48.6 2003   min_rew   0.07035740 #> 7   c3 6.4 48.6 2003  NJstress 127.00000000 #> 8   c3 6.4 48.6 2003   Istress  80.57571622 #> 9   c3 6.4 48.6 2003   min_rew   0.04590300 #> 10  c4 6.6 48.6 2003  NJstress 139.00000000 #> 11  c4 6.6 48.6 2003   Istress 102.06302437 #> 12  c4 6.6 48.6 2003   min_rew   0.02345772 #> 13  c5 6.0 48.7 2003  NJstress  98.00000000 #> 14  c5 6.0 48.7 2003   Istress  49.98710783 #> 15  c5 6.0 48.7 2003   min_rew   0.08972377 #> 16  c6 6.2 48.7 2003  NJstress 115.00000000 #> 17  c6 6.2 48.7 2003   Istress  62.24641800 #> 18  c6 6.2 48.7 2003   min_rew   0.07035740 #> 19  c7 6.4 48.7 2003  NJstress 127.00000000 #> 20  c7 6.4 48.7 2003   Istress  80.57571622 #> 21  c7 6.4 48.7 2003   min_rew   0.04590300 #> 22  c8 6.6 48.7 2003  NJstress 139.00000000 #> 23  c8 6.6 48.7 2003   Istress 102.06302437 #> 24  c8 6.6 48.7 2003   min_rew   0.02345772 #> 25  c9 6.0 48.8 2003  NJstress  98.00000000 #> 26  c9 6.0 48.8 2003   Istress  49.98710783 #> 27  c9 6.0 48.8 2003   min_rew   0.08972377 #> 28 c10 6.2 48.8 2003  NJstress 115.00000000 #> 29 c10 6.2 48.8 2003   Istress  62.24641800 #> 30 c10 6.2 48.8 2003   min_rew   0.07035740 #> 31 c11 6.4 48.8 2003  NJstress 127.00000000 #> 32 c11 6.4 48.8 2003   Istress  80.57571622 #> 33 c11 6.4 48.8 2003   min_rew   0.04590300 #> 34 c12 6.6 48.8 2003  NJstress 139.00000000 #> 35 c12 6.6 48.8 2003   Istress 102.06302437 #> 36 c12 6.6 48.8 2003   min_rew   0.02345772 head(grille) #> <biljou_grid>: 2 points, 1 year(s), 3 indicator(s) #> Indicators: NJstress, Istress, min_rew  #>   id lon  lat year indicator        value #> 1 c1 6.0 48.6 2003  NJstress  98.00000000 #> 2 c1 6.0 48.6 2003   Istress  49.98710783 #> 3 c1 6.0 48.6 2003   min_rew   0.08972377 #> 4 c2 6.2 48.6 2003  NJstress 115.00000000 #> 5 c2 6.2 48.6 2003   Istress  62.24641800 #> 6 c2 6.2 48.6 2003   min_rew   0.07035740 nj <- grille[grille$indicator == "NJstress", c("id", "lon", "lat", "value")] nj[order(-nj$value), ] #> <biljou_grid>: 12 points, 0 year(s), 0 indicator(s) #> Indicators:   #>     id lon  lat value #> 10  c4 6.6 48.6   139 #> 22  c8 6.6 48.7   139 #> 34 c12 6.6 48.8   139 #> 7   c3 6.4 48.6   127 #> 19  c7 6.4 48.7   127 #> 31 c11 6.4 48.8   127 #> 4   c2 6.2 48.6   115 #> 16  c6 6.2 48.7   115 #> 28 c10 6.2 48.8   115 #> 1   c1 6.0 48.6    98 #> 13  c5 6.0 48.7    98 #> 25  c9 6.0 48.8    98

title: Cartographie de la sécheresse avec SAFRAN
text: Une carte simple des points colorés par le nombre de jours de stress, sans dépendance supplémentaire :  Le gradient ouest → est (de plus en plus sec) se traduit bien par un nombre croissant de jours de stress.
code: val <- nj$value pal <- hcl.colors(10, "YlOrRd", rev = TRUE) col <- pal[cut(val, breaks = 10, labels = FALSE)] plot(nj$lon, nj$lat, pch = 15, cex = 4, col = col,      xlab = "Longitude", ylab = "Latitude",      main = "Jours de stress (NJstress) - exemple synthetique") text(nj$lon, nj$lat, round(val), cex = 0.8)

what: 4. Carte raster avec terra / couche sf
title: Cartographie de la sécheresse avec SAFRAN
text: Quand terra (ou sf) est installé, obtient une vraie sortie SIG, cartographiable et exportable (GeoTIFF, etc.). Ces chunks ne s’exécutent que si le package est présent.
code: r <- biljou_grid_to_raster(grille, indicator = "NJstress", year = 2003) r #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 3, 4, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 0.2, 0.1  (x, y) #> extent      : 5.9, 6.7, 48.55, 48.85  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : lon/lat WGS 84 (EPSG:4326) #> source(s)   : memory #> name        : NJstress #> min value   :       98 #> max value   :      139 terra::plot(r, main = "NJstress (2003)") # terra::writeRaster(r, "njstress_2003.tif")  # export GeoTIFF pts_sf <- biljou_grid_to_sf(grille, indicator = "NJstress", year = 2003) pts_sf #> Simple feature collection with 12 features and 4 fields #> Geometry type: POINT #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 6 ymin: 48.6 xmax: 6.6 ymax: 48.8 #> Geodetic CRS:  WGS 84 #> First 10 features: #>     id year indicator value         geometry #> 1   c1 2003  NJstress    98   POINT (6 48.6) #> 4   c2 2003  NJstress   115 POINT (6.2 48.6) #> 7   c3 2003  NJstress   127 POINT (6.4 48.6) #> 10  c4 2003  NJstress   139 POINT (6.6 48.6) #> 13  c5 2003  NJstress    98   POINT (6 48.7) #> 16  c6 2003  NJstress   115 POINT (6.2 48.7) #> 19  c7 2003  NJstress   127 POINT (6.4 48.7) #> 22  c8 2003  NJstress   139 POINT (6.6 48.7) #> 25  c9 2003  NJstress    98   POINT (6 48.8) #> 28 c10 2003  NJstress   115 POINT (6.2 48.8) # plot(pts_sf["value"])

title: Cartographie de la sécheresse avec SAFRAN
text: Avec une grille SAFRAN réelle (étape 1) à la place de la météo synthétique (étape 2), cette même chaîne produit des cartes nationales d’indicateurs de sécheresse comparables à celles de l’outil BILJOU en ligne — en choisissant l’indicateur (NJstress, Istress, DEBstress, min_rew…) et l’année voulus.

what: biljouR - modèle de bilan hydrique forestier BILJOU en R
title: biljouR - modèle de bilan hydrique forestier BILJOU en R
text: 📖 Documentation et articles : https://pobsteta.github.io/biljouR Réimplémentation indépendante en R du modèle de bilan hydrique forestier BILJOU (Granier, Bréda, Biron & Villette, 1999, Ecological Modelling 116:269-283), tel que documenté par l’INRAE UMR Silva (https://appgeodb.nancy.inrae.fr/biljou/). ⚠️ Ce package est une réimplémentation indépendante, à des fins de recherche et d’enseignement. Il n’est ni produit ni cautionné par l’INRAE. Pour des simulations de référence, utilisez l’outil officiel en ligne.

title: biljouR - modèle de bilan hydrique forestier BILJOU en R
text: Calcule, au pas de temps journalier, le bilan hydrique du sol ΔW = P − − T − Eu − D d’un peuplement forestier et renvoie les flux journaliers, le contenu en eau du sol, la réserve en eau relative (REW) et les indicateurs de sécheresse (nombre de jours de stress, indice de stress Istress, intensité, précocité).

title: biljouR - modèle de bilan hydrique forestier BILJOU en R
text: Depuis GitHub (recommandé) : Ou depuis une archive source locale :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("pobsteta/biljouR")  # avec la vignette : remotes::install_github("pobsteta/biljouR", build_vignettes = TRUE) install.packages("chemin/vers/biljouR_0.1.0.tar.gz", repos = NULL, type = "source")

title: biljouR - modèle de bilan hydrique forestier BILJOU en R
code: library(biljouR) data(meteo_hesse)  sol <- biljou_soil(   ewm   = c(70, 70, 40),   # mm d'eau extractible par couche   roots = c(0.6, 0.3, 0.1) # fractions de racines fines )  run <- biljou_run(meteo_hesse, sol, lai_max = 6,                   forest_type = "broadleaved",   # ou "coniferous"                   budburst = 110, leaf_fall = 300)  run                       # résumé biljou_indices(run)       # indicateurs annuels (NJstress, Istress, DEBstress) biljou_annual_balance(run)# bilan annuel des flux plot(run)                 # REW + flux journaliers

title: biljouR - modèle de bilan hydrique forestier BILJOU en R
text: Météo (journalière) : pet (mm, Penman) et rain (mm). La fonction penman_pet() calcule l’ETP Penman à partir de la température, du rayonnement, du vent et de l’humidité si besoin. Peuplement : lai_max, forest_type (et dates de débourrement / chute des feuilles si décidu). Sol : 1 à 3 couches, chacune avec son eau extractible maximale, sa fraction de racines fines et, optionnellement, sa macro-/microporosité.

what: Correspondance avec les sorties de l’outil en ligne
title: biljouR - modèle de bilan hydrique forestier BILJOU en R
text: En résumé : le package reproduit désormais les sorties numériques ET graphiques du modèle (tables journalières/annuelles, indicateurs, classement, graphiques, statistiques) et fournit le pipeline cartographique pour produire des cartes de type BILJOU. Seules restent hors périmètre l’interface web et les cartes nationales pré-calculées (mais elles sont reproductibles avec biljou_run_grid() sur une grille SAFRAN).

title: biljouR - modèle de bilan hydrique forestier BILJOU en R
text: Pour produire des cartes comme l’outil en ligne, il faut une météo en points de grille. Les données SAFRAN (réanalyse Météo-France, maille 8×8 km, journalier, 1958→présent) sont gratuites depuis 2024. Pour ce package, le miroir NetCDF par variable (INRAE, DOI 10.57745/BAZ12C) est la source la plus pratique : ne télécharge que les variables utiles (PRELIQ_Q, PRENEI_Q, ETP_Q), une fois, puis extrait tous les points en lecture paresseuse. Le package fournit des fonctions dédiées : Autres voies d’accès aux mêmes données SAFRAN : meteo.data.gouv.fr — jeu « Données changement climatique – SIM quotidienne » : CSV compressés par lots de 1 à 10 ans (à lire puis convertir avec safran_to_meteo()). https://meteo.data.gouv.fr API OGC EDR SAFRAN (1958→aujourd’hui) pour des requêtes ciblées par emprise/période — plus léger pour une simulation ponctuelle. Alternative mondiale : ERA5-Land (≈9 km) via le package R ecmwfr. Mention obligatoire à la diffusion : « Source : Météo-France ». La géométrie de la grille SAFRAN peut varier selon les produits : inspectez un fichier avec terra::rast() et ajustez le mapping de safran_nc_to_meteo() si besoin.
code: # 1. lister et télécharger les NetCDF des variables utiles (API Dataverse) files <- safran_download(variables = c("PRELIQ_Q","PRENEI_Q","ETP_Q"),                          dest_dir = "safran")   # -> chemins locaux nommés  # 2. extraire la météo par point depuis les NetCDF (terra) points <- data.frame(id = c("p1","p2"), lon = c(6.0,6.2), lat = c(48.6,48.8)) meteo_list <- safran_nc_to_meteo(files, points)  # liste de data.frames par point  # 3. lancer le modèle sur la grille, puis cartographier grid <- biljou_run_grid(points, meteo = meteo_list,                         soil = biljou_soil(140), lai_max = 5,                         forest_type = "broadleaved",                         budburst = 110, leaf_fall = 300,                         indicators = "NJstress") r <- biljou_grid_to_raster(grid, indicator = "NJstress", year = 2003) terra::plot(r)

title: biljouR - modèle de bilan hydrique forestier BILJOU en R
text: Certaines constantes ne sont décrites que qualitativement dans la documentation publique (partage macro/microporosité, pondération du prélèvement racinaire, coefficient d’évaporation du sous-étage, comportement de l’interception aux fortes pluies). Les choix faits ici sont transparents et documentés dans le code et la vignette, et devraient être calés sur l’outil officiel avant tout usage en production.

title: biljouR - modèle de bilan hydrique forestier BILJOU en R
text: GPL-3.

title: biljouR: a daily forest water balance model — biljouR-package
text: Main functions: biljou_soil, biljou_run, biljou_indices, biljou_annual_balance, penman_pet. package independent re-implementation research teaching produced endorsed INRAE. authoritative simulations use official tool https://appgeodb.nancy.inrae.fr/biljou/.

title: biljouR: a daily forest water balance model — biljouR-package
text: Granier , Breda N, Biron P, Villette S (1999) lumped water balance model evaluate duration intensity drought constraints forest stands. Ecological Modelling 116:269-283.

text: Sums elementary water fluxes calendar year \(P \approx + T + Eu + D + \Delta W\) can checked.

what: Drought indices from a BILJOU run — biljou_indices
title: Drought indices from a BILJOU run — biljou_indices
text: Per-year ecophysiological drought indicators, accumulated leafed period deciduous stands whole year evergreen stands.

title: Drought indices from a BILJOU run — biljou_indices
code: biljou_indices(run)

title: Drought indices from a BILJOU run — biljou_indices
text: run biljou_run object.

title: Drought indices from a BILJOU run — biljou_indices
text: data frame, one row per year, BILJOU annual-file indicators NJstress (number stress days), Istress (\(\sum (0.4 - REW)/0.4\), online-tool definition), DEBstress (day--year drought onset), plus min_rew, is_1999 (\(\sum (0.4 - REW)\), Granier et al. 1999 Eq. 5) flux totals.

title: Drought indices from a BILJOU run — biljou_indices
text: Granier , Breda N, Biron P, Villette S (1999) Ecological Modelling 116:269-283.

what: Daily leaf area index from phenology — biljou_lai
title: Daily leaf area index from phenology — biljou_lai
text: Coniferous stands keep constant LAI; deciduous stands ramp 0 lai_max 30 days budburst back 0 30 days leaf fall.

title: Daily leaf area index from phenology — biljou_lai
code: biljou_lai(doy, lai_max, forest_type = c("broadleaved", "coniferous"),   budburst = NULL, leaf_fall = NULL, ramp = 30)

title: Daily leaf area index from phenology — biljou_lai
text: doy Integer vector day--year values (1-366). lai_max Maximum (plateau) leaf area index. forest_type "broadleaved" (deciduous) "coniferous" (evergreen). budburst Day--year budburst (deciduous ). leaf_fall Day--year complete leaf fall (deciduous ). ramp Duration leaf expansion/senescence ramp, days (default 30).

title: Daily leaf area index from phenology — biljou_lai
text: Numeric vector daily LAI.

what: Overlay years by day-of-year (inter-annual comparison) — biljou_plot_overlay
title: Overlay years by day-of-year (inter-annual comparison) — biljou_plot_overlay

title: Overlay years by day-of-year (inter-annual comparison) — biljou_plot_overlay
code: biljou_plot_overlay(run, var = "REW", stat = c("median","mean","none"))

title: Overlay years by day-of-year (inter-annual comparison) — biljou_plot_overlay
text: run biljou_run object. var single variable alias (default "REW"). stat Optional summary curve: "median", "mean" "none".

title: Overlay years by day-of-year (inter-annual comparison) — biljou_plot_overlay
text: ggplot2 object.

what: Time series of daily fluxes / states (multi-year chronicle) — biljou_plot_timeseries
title: Time series of daily fluxes / states (multi-year chronicle) — biljou_plot_timeseries

title: Time series of daily fluxes / states (multi-year chronicle) — biljou_plot_timeseries

title: Rank years by drought severity — biljou_rank_droughts

title: Rank years by drought severity — biljou_rank_droughts

title: Rank years by drought severity — biljou_rank_droughts

what: Run the BILJOU daily water balance — biljou_run
title: Run the BILJOU daily water balance — biljou_run
text: Iterates \(\Delta W = P - - T - Eu - D\) (Granier et al. 1999, Eq. 1) meteorological series following BILJOU flow chart.

title: Run the BILJOU daily water balance — biljou_run
code: biljou_run(meteo, soil, lai_max,   forest_type = c("broadleaved", "coniferous"),   budburst = NULL, leaf_fall = NULL, rew_c = 0.4, k = 0.5,   et_max_ratio = 1.2, wet_canopy_factor = 0.2, interception_coef = NULL)

title: Run the BILJOU daily water balance — biljou_run
text: meteo Data frame columns date (doy), pet (mm) rain (mm), one row per day. soil biljou_soil object. lai_max Maximum leaf area index. forest_type "broadleaved" "coniferous". budburst, leaf_fall Day--year budburst leaf fall (deciduous). rew_c Critical relative extractable water (default 0.4). k Light extinction coefficient (default 0.5). et_max_ratio Cap actual ET multiple PET (default 1.2). wet_canopy_factor Fraction interception subtracted transpiration (0.2). interception_coef Optional interception coefficient override.

title: Run the BILJOU daily water balance — biljou_run
text: object class biljou_run element daily.

title: Run the BILJOU daily water balance — biljou_run
text: Granier , Breda N, Biron P, Villette S (1999) Ecological Modelling 116:269-283.

title: Run the BILJOU daily water balance — biljou_run

what: Run BILJOU over a grid of points (cartographic pipeline) — biljou_run_grid
title: Run BILJOU over a grid of points (cartographic pipeline) — biljou_run_grid
text: Applies biljou_run grid point extracts annual drought indicators, build BILJOU-style maps gridded climate dataset (e.g. SAFRAN).

title: Run BILJOU over a grid of points (cartographic pipeline) — biljou_run_grid
code: biljou_run_grid(points, meteo, soil, lai_max, forest_type,   budburst = NULL, leaf_fall = NULL,   indicators = c("NJstress","Istress","DEBstress","min_rew","drainage"),   years = NULL, id_col = "id", lon_col = "lon", lat_col = "lat",   verbose = TRUE, ...)

title: Run BILJOU over a grid of points (cartographic pipeline) — biljou_run_grid
text: points Data frame id coordinate columns. meteo Per-point meteo: function(id), named list, one data frame . soil biljou_soil, function(id) named list. lai_max, forest_type, budburst, leaf_fall Single values functions id. indicators Annual indicators keep (see biljou_indices). years Optional years keep. id_col, lon_col, lat_col Column names points. verbose Print progress. ... Passed biljou_run.

title: Run BILJOU over a grid of points (cartographic pipeline) — biljou_run_grid
text: biljou_grid data frame: id, lon, lat, year, indicator, value.

title: Define a soil profile — biljou_soil
text: Describes 1-3 layer soil profile used biljou_run.

title: Define a soil profile — biljou_soil
code: biljou_soil(ewm, roots = NULL, macro = NULL, micro = NULL, init = 1)

title: Define a soil profile — biljou_soil
text: ewm Maximum extractable water per layer (mm), length 1-3. roots Fine-root fractions per layer (sum 1); default proportional ewm. macro, micro Optional macro-/micro-porosity per layer controlling fast-bypass fraction macro/(macro+micro) infiltration. init Initial relative extractable water (0-1) per layer (default 1).

title: Define a soil profile — biljou_soil
text: object class biljou_soil.

what: Synthetic daily meteorology (one temperate year) — meteo_hesse
title: Synthetic daily meteorology (one temperate year) — meteo_hesse
text: one-year synthetic daily meteorological series dry summer, used examples. real measurements.

code: data(meteo_hesse)

title: Synthetic daily meteorology (one temperate year) — meteo_hesse
text: data frame 365 rows 4 columns: date, doy, pet (mm/day), rain (mm/day).

title: Penman potential evapotranspiration — penman_pet
text: Penman (1948) combination equation, Shuttleworth (1993) formulation, FAO-56 net radiation. BILJOU calibrated Penman PET.

title: Penman potential evapotranspiration — penman_pet
code: penman_pet(tmean, rg, wind, rh = NULL, ea = NULL, vpd = NULL,   doy = NULL, latitude = NULL, altitude = 0, albedo = 0.2)

title: Penman potential evapotranspiration — penman_pet
text: tmean Mean air temperature (deg C). rg Incident global radiation (MJ m-2 day-1; multiply J cm-2 0.01). wind Wind speed 2 m (m s-1). rh Mean relative humidity (%); supply ea vpd. ea Actual vapour pressure (kPa). vpd Vapour pressure deficit (kPa). doy Day year (longwave term). latitude Latitude (decimal degrees, longwave term). altitude Elevation (m, default 0). albedo Surface albedo (default 0.2).

title: Penman potential evapotranspiration — penman_pet
text: PET (mm day-1).

title: Penman potential evapotranspiration — penman_pet
text: Penman (1948); Shuttleworth (1993); Allen et al. (1998) FAO-56.

title: Penman potential evapotranspiration — penman_pet
code: penman_pet(tmean = 18, rg = 22, wind = 2, rh = 65,            doy = 180, latitude = 48.6, altitude = 250) #> [1] 5.081286

title: Daily potential overstorey transpiration — potential_transpiration
text: \(T_p = r(LAI) \times PET\). Stress wet-canopy reductions applied biljou_run.

title: Daily potential overstorey transpiration — potential_transpiration
code: potential_transpiration(pet, lai, r_max = 0.75, lai_threshold = 6)

title: Daily potential overstorey transpiration — potential_transpiration
text: pet Potential evapotranspiration (mm). lai Leaf area index. r_max, lai_threshold See transpiration_ratio.

title: Daily potential overstorey transpiration — potential_transpiration
text: Potential transpiration (mm).

title: Potential understorey + soil evaporation — potential_understorey_evap
text: Proportional energy reaching floor: \(Eu_p = PET \times exp(-k\,LAI)\). Soil-water limitation applied biljou_run.

title: Potential understorey + soil evaporation — potential_understorey_evap
code: potential_understorey_evap(pet, lai, k = 0.5)

title: Potential understorey + soil evaporation — potential_understorey_evap
text: pet Potential evapotranspiration (mm). lai Leaf area index. k Light extinction coefficient (default 0.5).

title: Potential understorey + soil evaporation — potential_understorey_evap
text: Potential understorey + soil evaporation (mm).

what: Fraction of incident radiation transmitted through the canopy — radiation_transmittance
title: Fraction of incident radiation transmitted through the canopy — radiation_transmittance
text: Beer-Lambert extinction \(R/R0 = exp(-k\,LAI)\).

title: Fraction of incident radiation transmitted through the canopy — radiation_transmittance
code: radiation_transmittance(lai, k = 0.5)

title: Fraction of incident radiation transmitted through the canopy — radiation_transmittance
text: lai Leaf area index (m2 m-2). k Light extinction coefficient (default 0.5).

title: Fraction of incident radiation transmitted through the canopy — radiation_transmittance
text: Transmitted fraction [0, 1].

what: Daily rainfall interception (Granier et al. 1999, Eq. 3) — rainfall_interception
title: Daily rainfall interception (Granier et al. 1999, Eq. 3) — rainfall_interception
text: Throughfall \(Th = exp(+ b\,R/R0 + c\,P + d\,P^2)\) \(= P - Th\), Aussenac (1968). \(R/R0\) percent transmitted radiation \(100\,exp(-k\,LAI)\). clamped [0, P].

title: Daily rainfall interception (Granier et al. 1999, Eq. 3) — rainfall_interception
code: rainfall_interception(p, lai, forest_type = c("broadleaved", "coniferous"),   k = 0.5, coef = NULL, min_rain = NULL)

title: Daily rainfall interception (Granier et al. 1999, Eq. 3) — rainfall_interception
text: p Incident rainfall (mm). lai Leaf area index. forest_type "broadleaved" "coniferous". k Light extinction coefficient (default 0.5). coef Optional named ,b,c,d overriding default coefficients. min_rain Threshold = P (default 1 broadleaved, 2 coniferous).

title: Daily rainfall interception (Granier et al. 1999, Eq. 3) — rainfall_interception
text: Interception (mm).

title: Daily rainfall interception (Granier et al. 1999, Eq. 3) — rainfall_interception
text: Aussenac G (1968) Ann. Sci. . 25:135-156.

title: Download SAFRAN NetCDF variable files — safran_download

title: Read SAFRAN NetCDF files at grid points into per-point meteo — safran_nc_to_meteo
text: files Named character vector NetCDF paths (names = variable codes). points Data frame points id lon/lat columns. id_col, lon_col, lat_col Column names points. pts_crs CRS point coordinates (default EPSG:4326). cols Mapping passed safran_to_meteo.

title: Convert SAFRAN / SIM quotidienne data to the biljou meteo format — safran_to_meteo

title: Convert SAFRAN / SIM quotidienne data to the biljou meteo format — safran_to_meteo
text: data frame date, doy, rain, pet.

text: u Wind speed measured height z (m s-1). z Measurement height (m).

title: Adjust wind speed to a 2 m reference height — wind_to_2m
text: Wind speed 2 m (m s-1).

title: biljouR 0.1.0
text: First release. Daily forest water balance simulation biljou_run() implementing ΔW = P − − T − Eu − D (Granier et al. 1999, Eq. 1). Stand phenology / daily LAI (biljou_lai()): constant evergreen stands, 30-day expansion senescence ramps deciduous stands. Overstorey transpiration r = T/PET relationship (transpiration_ratio(), potential_transpiration(); Eq. 2), wet-canopy reduction water-stress regulation REWc = 0.4. Rainfall interception Aussenac (1968) (rainfall_interception(); Eq. 3) stand-type coefficients. Understorey + soil evaporation transmitted radiation (potential_understorey_evap()). 1–3 layer soil (biljou_soil()) macro-/micro-porosity fast-bypass drainage root- REW-weighted water uptake. Drought indices per year (biljou_indices()) using online tool’s annual-file names definitions: NJstress (stress days), Istress (), DEBstress (onset day); un-normalised Granier et al. 1999 index (Eq. 5) also returned is_1999. biljou_rank_droughts(): ranks years duration, intensity precocity (BILJOU “classified drought years” output). Graphics module (requires ggplot2): biljou_plot_timeseries() (multi-year chronicle / faceted ETP, ETR, T, Eu, drainage, REW) biljou_plot_overlay() (year overlay day--year mean/median curve). biljou_doy_stats(): inter-annual mean/median/quantiles per day--year. Cartographic pipeline: biljou_run_grid() runs model grid points; biljou_grid_to_sf() biljou_grid_to_raster() export results (require sf / terra). safran_to_meteo(): converts Meteo-France SAFRAN / SIM quotidienne data meteo input format. SAFRAN NetCDF mirror access (INRAE Dataverse, DOI 10.57745/BAZ12C): safran_dataverse_files() lists per-variable NetCDF files, safran_download() downloads variables BILJOU needs, safran_nc_to_meteo() extracts per-point meteo (require jsonlite / terra). Annual water-balance summary (biljou_annual_balance()). Penman potential evapotranspiration helper (penman_pet()) 2 m wind adjustment (wind_to_2m()). Synthetic example dataset meteo_hesse, test suite, introductory vignette. package independent re-implementation research teaching produced endorsed INRAE.
